shapes.title: Target table not found
shapes.description: ADD COLUMN, ALTER COLUMN, DROP COLUMN, or similar operation references a table that does not exist in the replayed schema state.

corpus_suitability.reason: Production-merged filters out hallucinated table references

shapes.title: Target column not found
shapes.description: Operation references a column that does not exist on the target table in the replayed schema state.

corpus_suitability.reason: Production-merged filters out hallucinated column references

shapes.title: Foreign key references unknown table or column
shapes.description: FK constraint references a table or column that does not exist in the replayed schema state.

corpus_suitability.reason: Production-merged filters out unknown FK targets

shapes.title: Create target already exists
shapes.description: CREATE TABLE or CREATE INDEX targets a name that already exists in the replayed schema state.

corpus_suitability.reason: Production-merged filters out duplicate create attempts

shapes.title: Rename source missing or target conflict
shapes.description: RENAME TABLE or RENAME COLUMN source does not exist, or the target name already exists.

corpus_suitability.reason: Production-merged filters out broken rename operations

shapes.title: DROP COLUMN with FK dependents
shapes.description: DROP COLUMN targets a column that has incoming foreign key references from other tables, which will cascade or fail at runtime.

tier_history.reason: calibration attempted, produced 0 findings on prisma-production-v1, below 10-finding floor

calibration attempted on django-readthedocs-v1 via new Django AST parser, produced 0 canonical incoming-FK findings and 4 outgoing-FK-sub-case findings (all FP due to parser not modeling Django's implicit DROP CONSTRAINT before DROP COLUMN); 4 below the 10-finding floor

corpus_suitability.reason: Prisma migration generator emits DROP CONSTRAINT before DROP COLUMN, so by the time DROP COLUMN runs the FK is already gone

Canonical DM-15 sub-case (incoming-FK dependents) produced 0 findings across 345 migrations; 72 RemoveField operations exist but none target a column with live incoming FK references at drop time. Outgoing-FK warning sub-case fires 4 times but all are parser-modeling artifacts, not true DM-15 failures — see attempt dm15-django-readthedocs-v1-2026-04-14 in attempts.jsonl.

shapes.title: DROP TABLE with FK dependents
shapes.description: DROP TABLE targets a table that has incoming foreign key references from other tables.

tier_history.reason: calibration attempted, produced 0 findings on prisma-production-v1, below 10-finding floor

corpus_suitability.reason: Prisma migration generator drops FK constraints before DROP TABLE, same defensive sequence as DM-15

shapes.title: Column type change is narrowing
shapes.description: ALTER COLUMN TYPE converts to a narrower type, causing potential data loss (BIGINT to INT, TEXT to VARCHAR(N), TIMESTAMPTZ to DATE, NUMERIC to FLOAT4).

corpus_suitability.reason: Prisma computes type changes through intermediate columns rather than direct narrowings

known_issues: Hardcoded narrowing-types table is incomplete. Needs expansion before calibration on any corpus.

shapes.title: NOT NULL without safe preconditions
shapes.description: ADD COLUMN x NOT NULL without DEFAULT, or ALTER COLUMN x SET NOT NULL where x is currently nullable and has no default. Will fail on any non-empty table at runtime.

tier_history.reason: 19/0 precision on prisma-production-v1, clears 80% blocking threshold and 10-finding sample floor

corpus_suitability.reason: NOT NULL without DEFAULT is a runtime data failure that no generator can pre-empt and code review cannot catch without knowing row counts. Fired on 19 of 761 migrations (2.5% base rate).

provenance_notes: DM-18's classification work (recorded in dad8a42 at 09:29 on 2026-04-12) predates the formal calibration bar commit (2b5d1fb at 17:20 on 2026-04-12) by approximately seven hours and fifty-one minutes. DM-18 is therefore calibrated under a retroactively-formalized bar. The promotion decision matches what the bar would have required (19/19 = 100% clears every threshold), but the procedural pre-registration requirement of Component 1 was not satisfied for this specific claim. This gap is acknowledged in commit 51d767d (2026-04-13). The first shape measured under a fully pre-registered bar is DM-15.

shapes.title: DROP INDEX backing a constraint
shapes.description: DROP INDEX targets an index that is the backing index for a UNIQUE or PRIMARY KEY constraint, which would break the constraint invariant.

corpus_suitability.reason: Prisma manages indexes through named constraint definitions, so DROP INDEX on a constraint-backing index is pre-empted

known_issues: Index-name matching is fragile. Needs hardening before calibration on any corpus.
